sidecars.label: OpenHands
sidecars.truth_surface_owner: Athanor dashboard, atlas, and routing contracts
keep_if: OpenHands proves unique value on bounded worker tasks without creating a second control plane.
Worker outputs remain reproducible, reviewable, and easy to demote back to native lanes.
Its tool and MCP posture stays within Athanor policy and repo-sensitivity contracts.
demote_if: It matches existing Codex or Aider outcomes without offering meaningful worker-lane leverage.
The operator cost of configuration, session state, or auth drift outweighs the measured benefit.
The pilot needs ongoing exceptions to fit inside current routing or approval rules.
remove_if: It starts accumulating competing orchestration truth outside Athanor registries and packets.
It requires broad default access instead of explicit bounded-worker selection.
A simpler native lane wins the same eval slice with lower overhead.

sidecars.label: Roo Code
sidecars.truth_surface_owner: Athanor dashboard, atlas, and routing contracts
keep_if: Roo adds IDE-native value that is hard to reproduce through Goose or direct CLI work.
Its MCP and model-routing posture remains bounded by Athanor sensitivity and vendor-policy rules.
demote_if: It duplicates terminal-first flows without improving throughput or review quality.
Its IDE-only posture makes it less useful than the primary dashboard plus terminal front door.
remove_if: It causes state drift, operator confusion, or duplicate shell/control-plane behavior.
It no longer earns a place beyond occasional editor convenience.

sidecars.label: Augment Context Engine
sidecars.truth_surface_owner: Athanor dashboard, atlas, and routing contracts
keep_if: Hosted indexing remains restricted to cloud-safe repos and improves context quality measurably.
The memory and indexing boundary stays legible relative to Athanor registry truth.
demote_if: Equivalent context quality is available from repo-native or local memory surfaces.
The hosted-indexing overhead or policy review burden outweighs the practical gain.
remove_if: It is needed for private, sovereign, or adult-sensitive work.
It starts acting like a universal context substrate instead of a bounded enhancer.

sidecars.label: Kimi Code
sidecars.truth_surface_owner: Athanor dashboard, atlas, and routing contracts
keep_if: The lane remains useful inside its vendor-limited personal-development scope.
It provides burst value without becoming part of the core execution doctrine.
demote_if: The scope restrictions leave too little practical room for meaningful routing.
Other subscription or local lanes cover the same work with fewer contractual constraints.
remove_if: Vendor-policy scope conflicts with the intended project or tenant usage.
The lane is mostly dead weight relative to the broader stack.

retiring_lanes.label: Claude Review Cloudsafe
retiring_lanes.truth_surface_owner: Athanor migration map and lane-selection matrix
keep_if: Claude still covers a review slice that does not yet have fresh replacement evidence.
demote_if: Gemini and Codex replacement coverage is fresh and passing for architecture review and final review.
remove_if: The migration-map exit gates are satisfied and two normal quota windows pass without emergency relay.
No current routing profile depends on Claude to close a required task class.
